en: That's a very simplistic view of it.
Give me the non-simplistic view.
Do you have three hours?
Yes.
The basic contention of empirical science
is that the universe is knowable,
and we are all made up of physical matter.
ko: 그리고 우리는 모두 물질로 이루어져 있습니다.

en: Including the human consciousness? Of course. Including whatever emotions we feel? Objection, Your Honor.
ko: 인간의 의식도 포함되나요? 물론입니다. 우리가 느끼는 감정도 포함되나요? 반대합니다, 재판장님.

en: I'm at a loss for what this has to do with anything.
The subject of this hearing is custody.
Professor Mercer argued that he was a good parent
ko: 머서 교수는 자신이 좋은 부모라고 주장했습니다.

en: and that my client was a bad parent
ko: 그리고 제 의뢰인은 나쁜 부모라고 했습니다.

en: based on the activities he videotaped.
I'm just trying to figure out the components of his good parenting.
ko: 그의 좋은 양육의 요소를 파악하려고 합니다.

en: By questioning his teachings? Yes.
Get specific fast, Counselor,
unless you want the objection sustained. Thank you, Your Honor.
ko: 그렇지 않으면 반대가 유지될 겁니다. 감사합니다, 재판장님.

en: Professor, do you believe that free will is a fiction? No, I... Chapter two on choice.
ko: 교수님, 자유 의지가 허구라고 생각하시나요? 아니요, 저는... 선택에 대한 두 번째 장입니다.

en: The physical interaction of our atoms
is the sine qua non of thoughts, feelings, actions.
We may believe we have free will, but...
ko: 우리는 자유 의지가 있다고 믿을 수 있지만...
